name: Nock
what_it_does: HTTP server mocking and expectations library for Node.js. Nock intercepts outgoing HTTP requests from your Node.js code and returns predefined responses without making real network calls. Enables unit testing of code that makes HTTP requests without requiring a real server or internet access. Works with http/https modules and most HTTP libraries (axios, node-fetch, got).
use_cases: Unit test code that calls external APIs without making real HTTP requests — mock responses for consistent, fast tests
Test error handling logic by intercepting HTTP calls and simulating 500 errors, timeouts, or network failures
Record and replay real HTTP interactions (nock.recorder) for integration test snapshots that don't require network access
Prevent accidental HTTP requests in test suites using nock.disableNetConnect() to catch unintended external calls
Test agent code that calls multiple external services by mocking each service's responses independently
not_for: Browser-based HTTP mocking — use Mock Service Worker (MSW) for browser environments
Load testing — Nock mocks requests, not a performance tool
End-to-end tests that should exercise real API behavior — save Nock for unit/integration tests only
best_when: You need to unit test Node.js code making HTTP calls without real network access — Nock is the standard Node.js HTTP mocking library.
avoid_when: You need browser HTTP mocking (use MSW), or you want tests that exercise real API behavior (use real API or SuperTest).

auth.notes: No authentication — testing library. Can mock auth endpoints by intercepting login requests.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Unmatched requests throw descriptive errors showing the expected vs actual request details. nock.pendingMocks() shows uncalled interceptors. Clear debugging output.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Mocks are consumed once by default — must re-setup between tests or use .persist() for reusable mocks. afterEach(nock.cleanAll) ensures clean state.
known_agent_gotchas: Nock interceptors are consumed once by default — if a test triggers multiple calls to the same endpoint, each call requires a separate interceptor setup or .times(n)
nock.cleanAll() must be called in afterEach or before each test — unconsumed interceptors bleed into subsequent tests causing confusing failures
Nock only intercepts Node.js http/https module — HTTP libraries using fetch() natively (Undici, node-fetch v3) may not be intercepted; verify compatibility
Request body matching is strict by default — request body must exactly match nock interceptor body; use match functions for partial matching
nock.disableNetConnect() is a powerful guard but must be re-enabled for any tests that need real network access
Nock doesn't support HTTP/2 or WebSocket mocking — only HTTP/1.1 is intercepted via the Node.js http module

security_score_components.security_notes: Testing library — no production security concerns. Prevents real API calls in test environments, reducing accidental data exposure.
